name: SSF
description:
SSF (Sound Set) files map sound-slot indices to TLK string IDs in KotOR I & II. Each slot (e.g. "battle cry 1", "attack 2", "pain") holds a TLK string ID; the game looks up that ID in dialog.tlk to get the actual sound filename (resref) to play. So an SSF defines which sounds a creature or object uses for combat, selection, pain, and other events.

The file is minimal: a 12-byte header (magic "SSF ", version, and an unknown u32 often 12), then a flat array of u32 TLK string IDs. The array has no length in the header—you derive the count from the file size: (fileSize − 12) / 4. Typically there are 28 slots (indices 0–27); the SSFType enum lists the meaning of each index (BATTLE_CRY_1, SELECT_1, ATTACK_1, … POISONED).

Parsing: Read the first 12 bytes (fileType, fileVersion, unknown). The remainder of the file is sound refs: (file.length − 12) / 4 u32 values, little-endian. Slot i (0–27) is the i-th u32; look it up in TLK to get the sound resref. If a slot is 0 or an invalid TLK index, no sound plays for that slot.

Writing: Build an array of 28 u32 TLK string IDs (one per SSFType). Write the 12-byte header, then the 28 u32s (112 bytes). File size will be 124 bytes for a full sound set. All u32s are little-endian.

Header.description: 12 bytes at the start. fileType is "SSF ", fileVersion is the format version. The last u32 is unused/unknown (often 12). The sound ref array starts at byte 12 and has no count in the header—compute count as (file size − 12) / 4.

fields.name: unknown
fields.description: Unknown purpose; often 12. Keep when round-tripping.

SoundRef.description: A TLK string ID. The game resolves this via dialog.tlk to get the sound resref (filename) to play. Slot index 0–27 corresponds to SSFType; the u32 at that position is the TLK ID for that slot.

SSFType.description: Sound set slot index (0–27). The SSF file stores one u32 per slot in this order. Index 0 = BATTLE_CRY_1, 1 = BATTLE_CRY_2, … 27 = POISONED. Use this enum to know which slot you're reading or writing when you index into the sound ref array.

sections.name: header
sections.description: Fixed 12-byte header. After this, the rest of the file is a contiguous array of u32 TLK string IDs. There is no length field—use (file size − 12) / 4 to get the number of slots.

sections.name: soundRefs
sections.description: Array of u32 sound refs (TLK string IDs). Count = (file size − 12) / 4; typically 28. Slot i is the i-th u32 (at offset 12 + i×4). Use SSFType to interpret each index (0 = BATTLE_CRY_1, etc.). Resolve each u32 via dialog.tlk to get the sound filename to play.
